title: npm publish prep — README, .npmignore, MIT license, package.json polish
description: Prepare @kntic/links for public npm publish. Write a complete README.md, polish package.json metadata, verify .npmignore is correct, and do a dry-run publish check. The README is the product's first impression — make it good.

features: Write README.md at repo root with: project tagline, install command (npm install -g @kntic/links), quick start (5 commands to go from zero to deployed), full command reference table, links.yaml schema reference, theme screenshots section (placeholder for now — add note: 'screenshots coming soon'), contributing section, MIT license badge
README tone: developer-first, direct, no corporate fluff. Show the commands, not adjectives.
README quick start should work end-to-end: links init → links add → links deploy --self → open dist/index.html

Polish package.json: add 'description' field, 'keywords' array (linktree, link-page, cli, privacy, self-hosted, developer), 'homepage' (https://kntic.ai), 'repository' (GitLab URL), 'bugs' URL, 'engines' (node >= 18), ensure 'files' array matches what should be published (src/, LICENSE, README.md — exclude .kntic/, skills/, Dockerfile.agent, kntic.yml)
Verify .npmignore or package.json 'files' field correctly excludes KNTIC orchestration files from the published package
Run `npm pack --dry-run` and verify the file list looks correct — only src/, package.json, README.md, LICENSE should be in the tarball
Add a `prepublishOnly` script in package.json that runs a basic sanity check (e.g., node -e 'import("./src/cli.js")'  or a simple lint)

notes:
[GIA REJECTED] Hook(s) failed: 001-adr-compliance.py. Alignment score 0.8333 below threshold 1.0000
This task should be the last one executed. It depends on all LINKS-00X tasks being merged. The agent should run npm pack --dry-run and include the output in the actions log to confirm the tarball contents are correct.
